question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a crafted HTTP request that causes the type assertion `next.(middleware.RouteMatcher)` to panic, crashing the Rosetta node mid-request and causing in-flight transaction history queries to be dropped or reordered in the response queue, leading to gaps in the transaction history visible to downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the Rosetta API with concurrent requests to exhaust the `requestInflightGauge` counter, causing the middleware to misrepresent the number of in-flight requests and indirectly starve legitimate block/transaction queries, resulting in reordered or incomplete transaction history delivery?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly GET `/metrics` to scrape `hiero_mirror_rosetta_request_duration` histogram data and infer the timing of concurrent transaction history requests, enabling them to predict and exploit race windows in block ordering logic?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a duplicate metric registration panic by sending requests that cause `init()` to be re-invoked in a test or plugin context, crashing the server and causing transaction history state to be lost or reset?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user inject arbitrary label values into `requestBytesHistogram` via crafted HTTP method strings (e.g., extremely long or special-character method names), causing Prometheus cardinality explosion that exhausts memory and disrupts the ordering of transaction history responses?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user craft requests with unusual HTTP methods to inject high-cardinality `method` label values into `requestDurationHistogram`, causing memory exhaustion that forces the Rosetta node to drop or reorder pending transaction history responses?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user access the unauthenticated `/metrics` endpoint to read `hiero_mirror_rosetta_request_inflight` gauge values and determine exactly when concurrent block/transaction queries are in-flight, then time a crafted request to collide with those queries and cause non-deterministic transaction ordering?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a large volume of requests with crafted route paths to inject high-cardinality `route` label values into `requestInflightGauge`, causing Prometheus registry memory exhaustion and forcing the server to restart, which resets in-flight transaction history state and causes history gaps?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user scrape `/metrics` to read `hiero_mirror_rosetta_response_bytes` histogram bucket data and infer which block/transaction endpoints are being queried by other users, enabling targeted interference with those queries to disrupt transaction history ordering?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of authentication on `MetricsMiddleware` instrumentation to send thousands of concurrent requests that saturate the `requestInflightGauge`, causing legitimate transaction history requests to be queued behind attacker requests and returned out of chronological order?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with crafted `route` label values that match existing Prometheus metric names, causing label collision in `requestBytesHistogram` and corrupting the metric registry state in a way that disrupts middleware routing for transaction history endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where `prometheus.DefaultRegisterer` is shared with another component and the `MustRegister` calls in `init()` panic due to duplicate registration, causing the Rosetta process to crash and lose the current transaction history processing state?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request that causes the `middleware.Instrument.Wrap(next)` call to wrap the handler incorrectly due to a nil `RouteMatcher`, resulting in requests being routed to wrong handlers and transaction history being served from incorrect block ranges?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly poll the unauthenticated `/metrics` endpoint to monitor `hiero_mirror_rosetta_request_duration` bucket distributions and identify slow transaction history queries, then send competing requests during those slow windows to cause non-deterministic ordering of block responses?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with extremely long URL paths to inject unbounded `route` label strings into `responseBytesHistogram`, causing the Prometheus registry to allocate unbounded memory and crash the server, resetting transaction history processing state?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `requestInflightGauge` is a gauge (not a counter) to observe its value via `/metrics` and determine the exact moment when zero in-flight requests exist, then inject a burst of crafted block requests to be processed first, effectively prepending attacker-controlled queries to the transaction history response queue?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with crafted HTTP methods containing Prometheus label separator characters (e.g., `{`, `}`, `=`, `,`) to break the label encoding in `requestDurationHistogram`, corrupting metric state and causing the middleware to misroute subsequent transaction history requests?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the unauthenticated `/metrics` endpoint to enumerate all active routes via `hiero_mirror_rosetta_request_bytes` label values, then craft targeted DoS requests against specific transaction history routes to selectively suppress certain block ranges from being served?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `sizeBuckets` definition (`[]float64{512, 1024, 10*1024, 25*1024, 50*1024}`) by sending requests with body sizes precisely at bucket boundaries to cause histogram observation skew, which when read by monitoring systems creates a false picture of transaction history query patterns?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a stream of requests that keep `requestInflightGauge` permanently elevated, causing the Rosetta node's health checks to fail and triggering an automated restart that resets in-flight transaction history queries, creating a gap in the transaction history visible to downstream indexers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of rate limiting on the `MetricsMiddleware` instrumentation path to send millions of requests per second, causing the Prometheus histogram observation calls to become a CPU bottleneck that delays transaction history responses and causes them to be returned out of order?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent GET requests to `/metrics` while simultaneously sending block/transaction history requests, causing lock contention in `prometheus.DefaultRegisterer` that serializes transaction history responses and introduces artificial ordering delays?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request where the HTTP method is an empty string, causing the `method` label in `requestBytesHistogram` to be empty and potentially triggering a Prometheus validation error that panics the middleware and drops the associated transaction history request?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with Unicode or null-byte characters in the URL path to inject malformed `route` label values into `requestInflightGauge`, causing the Prometheus registry to behave unexpectedly and disrupt the tracking of in-flight transaction history requests?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `middleware.Instrument` wrapper's `RouteMatcher` dependency to send requests that match no defined route, causing the route label to be set to a catch-all value that pollutes `requestDurationHistogram` and masks the timing of legitimate transaction history queries?
